searchLedger.hypothesis: Partial-read framing and output serialization are shared with the reference path

searchActions.result: createFrameSplitter buffers until newline and uses StringDecoder for split UTF-8 at lines 74-100; writes serialize one JSON frame plus newline at lines 227-229, and output/socket queues serialize forwarding at lines 457-559. skill-advisor uses the same createSessionProxy path as spec-memory, differing only by classifier.

searchLedger.rationale: No skill-advisor-specific truncation/interleaving defect found in the message framing path. Partial reads, multibyte boundaries, and request/response tracking are handled in the shared proxy rather than reimplemented in the skill-advisor launcher.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Proven-equivalent shared implementation to mk-spec-memory for framing; no divergent skill-advisor bridge code on this path.

title: Dead-socket respawn decision is dropped, leaving the secondary with no bridge or JSON-RPC error
evidence: bridgeOrReportLeaseHeld returns maybeBridgeLeaseHolder(...) directly at lines 537-546, and checkStrictSingleWriter treats the awaited call as fully handled at lines 551-554 / 559-562 / 573-576. The shared bridge helper can return {action:"respawn"} when the lease-holder socket exists but fails deep liveness probes; mk-spec-memory handles that at bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:839-855 by calling respawnAfterDeadSocket, but mk-skill-advisor has no equivalent branch.
rationale: Reachable when the owner/lease pid is still live but the daemon IPC socket is dead or hung during a recycle. A secondary launcher enters the bridge path, gets a respawn verdict, then startup returns without spawning, bridging, or emitting a retryable JSON-RPC error. That is the old 'lease held / no connection' failure mode in a worse form because stdout can be empty.

title: Owner child exit clears leases and exits instead of relaunching a backend for bridged clients
evidence: The skill-advisor child exit handler clears all lease files and exits at lines 878-889. The spec-memory reference keeps the launcher alive and schedules relaunch on child exit at bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:1319-1345, with the owner stdio itself proxied through createSessionProxy at lines 1577-1583.
rationale: A bridged secondary can reconnect only if a backend returns on the same socket. On dist redeploy or daemon recycle, the skill-advisor owner launcher exits with the child, so the secondary proxy reattach loop eventually exhausts and closes instead of transparently reconnecting. It is bounded, not infinite, but it violates the cross-session reconnect contract.

title: Replayable set includes advisor calls with telemetry side effects
evidence: SKILL_ADVISOR_REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES includes advisor_recommend and advisor_validate at lines 171-178, and the classifier is wired into createSessionProxy at lines 203-209. On reconnect, launcher-session-proxy replays pending entries marked replayable at bin/lib/launcher-session-proxy.cjs:649-663. advisor_recommend appends shadow deltas, and advisor_validate can persist outcome records, so a commit-then-disconnect replay duplicates telemetry.
rationale: This does not corrupt JSON framing, but it makes reconnect replay non-idempotent for normal advisor traffic. A backend that processes the call and dies before the response reaches the proxy will cause duplicated shadow/outcome records after reattach.
